titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition
titlestmt.author: by H. Dean

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8124986.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 13, 2021 at 04:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition consists of material related to the life and career of the American poet Archibald MacLeish received by the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library after the processing of the Archibald MacLeish Collection acquired in 1976. The Addition consists of correspondence, manuscripts, photographs, personal papers, and sound recordings documenting MacLeish and his family between 1801 and 1995.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of William MacLeish, 1996 and 2006, and gift of A. Bruce MacLeish, 2016, 2017, and 2018. Other material came to the library in small quantities from various sources as indicated on individual folders.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Correspondence between Archibald MacLeish and Ishbel MacLeish (box 17) and Archibald MacLeish and Ada MacLeish (boxes 1- 14) in Series IV are restricted until 21 November 2045. For further information, consult the curator.
Boxes 43-46 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 76 (film): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Restricted Fragile Papers, housed in boxes 48-50, may be consulted only with the permission of the appropriate curator. Access copies of sound recordings for reference use have been substituted in the main files.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Archibald MacLeish (1892-1982)
bioghist.p: A biographical sketch of Archibald MacLeish can be found in the finding aid for the Archibald MacLeish Collection YCAL MSS 38.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition consists of material related to the life and career of the American poet Archibald MacLeish received by the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library after the processing of the Archibald MacLeish Collection acquired in 1976. The Addition contains correspondence, writings, personal papers, and sound recordings documenting Archibald and Ada MacLeish's personal life, family history, and careers. It also contains correspondence and personal papers documenting the personal and professional life of MacLeish's parents, Andrew MacLeish and Martha Hillard MacLeish, including letters from Archibald and Ada MacLeish as well as from Archibald's siblings Bruce, Ishbel, Kenneth, and Norman. Correspondence, writings, and objects documenting MacLeish's maternal ancestors, the Hillards, and correspondence to William MacLeish (Archibald and Ada MacLeish's son) are also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Archibald MacLeish Collection Addition is organized into ten series: I. Archibald and Ada MacLeish Correspondence, 1910-1982; II. Writings, 1915-1983; III. Personal Papers, 1892-1982; IV. MacLeish Family Papers, 1888-1968; V. Hillard Family Papers, 1801-1884; VI. William MacLeish Correspondence, 1979-1995; VII. Sound Recordings, 1960-1971; VIII. September 2016 acquisition, circa 1893-1980; IX. November 2017 acquisition, circa 1907-1949; and X. January 2018 acquisition circa 1888-1942.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged into three subseries: Letters between Ada and Archibald MacLeish, General Correspondence, and Condolence Letters. Some correspondents are present in both General Correspondence and Condolence Letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The correspondence documents the MacLeishs' relationship with each other as well as their relationships with family and friends. To a smaller extent the correspondence also evidences Archibald MacLeish's career.

did.unittitle: Letters between Ada and Archibald MacLeish

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 1-14: Restricted until 2045 November 21

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal for a film on Carl Sandburg and biography of Adato

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Bundy's speech at the International Publishers Association Congress

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of letters dating from 1924 to 1958 and undated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Emily Hitchcock to Kathryn Bell

did.unittitle: Independent Citizens' Committee of the Arts, Sciences and Professions

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2045 November 21

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relates to Hillard family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of letters from 1935 to 1964 (including 1929 letter from Dorothy Parker to Robert Benchley)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Writings are arranged into six subseries: Articles, Poetry, Playscripts, Speeches, Reviews, and Writings of Others. MacLeish's writings are arranged alphabetically by title and Writings of Others is arranged alphabetically by author (when known).

did.unittitle: "Lift the Ban", photocopy of printed version, The Nation 167.2
"Riders on the Earth Together, Brothers in Eternal Cold", printed excerpt from The New York Times
"What She Was Herself", photocopy of printed version, The New York Times

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Song by Richard E. Myers dedicated to Ada MacLeish with lyrics by Archibald MacLeish

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Yale Review (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale Publishing Association)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems printed in The Atlantic Monthly include: "Poet", "The Wood Dove at Sandy Spring", "And Out of Weakness Song"

did.unittitle: Tower of Ivory , drafts, holograph, corrected
"Voyage to the Moon", printed version from New York Times

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Tydeman's BBC radio adaptation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delivered at the fourteenth Cosmos Club Award, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delivered at University of California, Berkeley

National Medal for Literature award, typed excerpts

scopecontent.p: Delivered at the New York Public Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delivered at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis

did.unittitle: "The Poet and the Press", printed version

scopecontent.p: Reprinting in The Atlantic of MacLeish's speech "Poetry and Journalism"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delivered to the Arts Club in St. Augustine, Florida

did.unittitle: "Yeats and the Belief in Life", printed version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delivered at the University of New Hampshire

did.unittitle: Becker, Stephen, "On Being a Patient", The Atlantic Monthly
Benco, Nancy, "Archibald MacLeish: The Poet Librarian", The Quarterly Journal of the Library of Congress , 33.3
Cowley, Robert, "American Was Promises: An Interview with Archibald MacLeish", American Heritage 33.5
Cunningham, Phyllis Fenn, "My Aunt and Godmother: Miss Mary Robbins Hillard, Founder and First Principal of Westover School"
MacLeish, Rod, "Columbus Day Broadcast" typescript for WVDA ABC Radio in Boston

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Personal Papers are organized into three subseries: Diaries Scrapbooks, and Photographs, Other Papers, and Objects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album contains black and white photographs of Ada MacLeish's studies in France and travels in Europe and North Africa

did.unittitle: Scrapbook containing photographs of Ada and Archibald MacLeish
Scrapbook of Ada and Archibald MacLeish's trip to Japan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains souvenirs and black and white photographs of the MacLeishs' travels in Japan for Archibald MacLeish's work with Fortune Magazine

did.unittitle: Ada MacLeish's diary of Japan trip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes on medicine and expenses as well as drafts of poetry for Tower of Ivory

did.unittitle: Photographs of MacLeish family and friends
Photographs of MacLeish family and friends
did.physloc: Also stored in: Oversize box 41, folders 433-436 and box 42, folders 439-441.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portrait of Ada MacLeish by Man Ray

did.unittitle: Scrapbooks of family home Craigie Lea
Letters of recommendation for military service

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes black and white photograph of MacLeish

did.unittitle: Material relating to honorary degrees from University of Dubuque (Iowa) and University of Massachusetts
Concert Programs and Reviews documenting Ada MacLeish's career
Concert Posters for Ada MacLeish's performance while living in France
Papers and photographs relating to Kenneth MacLeish

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publication Kenneth: A Collection of Letters

did.unittitle: Bronze bust of Archibald MacLeish by Milton Hebald

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The MacLeish Family Papers are organized into three subseries: Correspondence, Writings, and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The MacLeish Family Papers contain correspondence, writings, and personal papers documenting the personal and professional life of Andrew MacLeish and Martha Hillard MacLeish (Archibald MacLeish's parents).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters transcribed originally written in 1918

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters transcribed originally written between 1917 and 1918

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typed and handwritten transcriptions of letters originally written between 1918 and 1920

did.unittitle: Certificates and clippings relating to Martha Hillard MacLeish

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Hillard Family Papers are arranged into two subseries: Correspondence and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Hillard Family Papers contain correspondence and personal papers documenting the lives of Archibald MacLeish's maternal ancestors.

did.unittitle: Partage de Héritages de feu Jean Helier Dumaresq

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three black and white photographs (one of Mary Ann Hillard and two of unidentified people)

did.unittitle: Hazeltine, Miron. Beadle's Dime Chess Instructor
Eraser, seal stamp, and ink well

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Archibald MacLeish's death certificate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains inscribed chapter from William MacLeish's memoir Uphill With Archie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of corrected typescript of Archibald MacLeish's poem "Whistler in the Dark"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript excerpts from Archibald MacLeish's journal from a trip to Persia in 1926 as well as a typescript copy of Archibald MacLeish's book Poems

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sound Recordings are organized into the following three subseries: Interviews, Plays, and Poetry.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Reference copies of sound recordings are filed in the folders.

"Conquistador" folio of original woodcut prints (Series II)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographic prints, photograph albums, printed material, and other papers relating to Archibald MacLeish and the MacLeish family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The September 2016 acquisition is organized into four subseries: Correspondence, Travel diaries and notebook, Photographs, and Other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings, correspondence, photographic prints and films relating to or by Archibald MacLeish and family.
